checks.a_analysis: I reviewed the output JSON against the source PDF. Each section and its constituent steps correspond directly to the content found in the original document. There are no fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or instructions. The metadata fields like 'title' and 'page_count' correctly reflect the document properties.

The only URL present in the document is 'https://myncid.nc.gov/', which appears multiple times across all three pages. I verified that this URL is captured accurately in the output JSON in every section where it appears. No other contact information (phone numbers or emails) exists in the source or the output.

The source document consists of three pages covering eight specific 'How to' topics. The output JSON contains sections for all eight topics: 'How to register', 'How to unlock your account', 'How to retrieve your username', 'How to reset your password', 'How to change your password', 'How to update your profile', 'How to enroll Open Subscription Applications', and 'How to delete your account'. All content from all three pages is accounted for.
The source document does not contain any tables; it is structured entirely as lists of instructions and illustrative images. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A detailed comparison confirms that all steps, notes, and labels within each section have been transferred to the JSON. While some sentences are split into list items and subsequent paragraph/image elements (e.g., when a button icon appears mid-sentence in the source), the complete semantic information is preserved.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output text is a faithful reproduction of the source text. It preserves source typos (e.g., the missing closing quote in Step 2 of 'How to unlock your account') and punctuation exactly as written. Minor variations in whitespace or the use of smart quotes are within acceptable reformatting limits. The omission of a specific label for the 'three-dot' icon in the 'How to delete your account' section is minor as the text flow remains understandable.

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly maps the content from the three pages of the source document into a single hierarchical structure. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs or sections found in the output that are not present in the source (e.g., intentional repetitions like 'Trouble Signing On?' buttons are correctly placed within their respective task sections).

The heading hierarchy is preserved and normalized. The document title 'MyNCID User Guide' is treated as the main section title (H1 equivalent), and the various 'How to...' tasks are correctly nested as subsections (H2 equivalent). No heading levels are skipped.
There are no tables in the source document to evaluate.

The reading order follows the source's multi-column layout logically, proceeding task-by-task (left-to-right, then top-to-bottom across pages). While some instructions are fragmented by interleaved images, the overall sequence of information is preserved.

The conversion fails to maintain the structural integrity of numbered lists. Continuous sequences in the source (e.g., 'How to register' steps 1-6) are split into multiple separate 'list' objects to accommodate interleaved images. Because the JSON 'text' field does not include the original numbers and the lists are sibling elements, the sequential numbering is lost. Additionally, in 'How to delete your account' step 3, the text is missing a reference to the menu icon ('Click on the in the upper right corner...'), creating a semantic gap in the list item.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1: 'How to register' steps 1-6. Page 3: 'How to delete your account' step 3: 'Click on the [vertical dots icon] in the upper right corner...'
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'How to register' section (reading_order 2) splits the 1-6 sequence into four separate 'list' elements. The 'How to delete your account' section (reading_order 9) has a list item text: 'Click on the in the upper right corner and click on “Delete my account”.'

checks.a_analysis: The source URLs (e.g., https://myncid.nc.gov/) are present as plain text in the output. While they are not specifically tagged as link objects in this JSON schema, the content is preserved and no fabricated links were added.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML is severely compromised. Instructional steps are separated from the UI elements (buttons) they refer to. Specifically, in the 'How to unlock your account' section, buttons like 'SAVE', 'CONFIRM', and 'Next' are inserted between steps and are assigned incorrect list numbers (restarting at 1), which breaks the logical flow and meaning of the guide. In the source, these buttons are inline with the steps in their respective sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF maintains a clear numbered sequence within columns where buttons are positioned directly after the instruction that refers to them (e.g., Page 1, 'How to register' steps 4 and 5).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML, the 'How to unlock your account' section contains a jumble of buttons ('Send Request', 'SAVE', 'CONFIRM', 'Next') interspersed with restarted numbered lists that do not match the source text or logic.
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML output, 'How to unlock your account' section

checks.a_analysis: There are no tables present in the source PDF or the converted HTML output.

Multiple meaningful images, including UI screenshots and functional buttons, are missing from the HTML output. They are replaced by generic green placeholder boxes or alt-text descriptions like 'Menu with options...', 'Send Request button', and 'Save button'. For example, the screenshots for 'Password' tab (Page 2) and 'Profile Information' tab (Page 2) are entirely absent, substituted by green blocks. This prevents a user from visually identifying the UI elements described in the text.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the source PDF, Page 2 shows screenshots of the 'Password' and 'Profile Information' tabs. Page 3 shows a screenshot of the 'delete' confirmation modal.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML shows large green boxes under 'How to reset your password' and 'How to update your profile'. It also displays text like 'Menu with options: Forgot Username, Forgot Password, Unlock Account' instead of the actual UI images.

checks.a_analysis: The content block listing 'Forgot Username', 'Forgot Password', and 'Unlock Account' is visually duplicated within the 'How to register' section of the HTML. Furthermore, this content is misplaced as it does not appear in the 'How to register' section of the source PDF at all.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 1, 'How to register' section contains no such menu list.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list 'Forgot Username / Forgot Password / Unlock Account' appears twice under the 'How to register' heading in the HTML.
